commands.author: Takhoffman
commands.repair_reason: automerge enabled; CHANGELOG.md entry is required before automerge; dispatch a focused changelog repair

actions.reason: adopted job must be committed before worker dispatch

target.title: fix(cron): use structured denial signals
target.body:
## Summary

- Remove cron outcome classification based on denial tokens found in summary, output text, final assistant text, or payload prose.
- Preserve fatal cron denial handling through typed `failureSignal` metadata sourced from structured tool error codes.
- Preserve thrown gateway/node-host denial codes across the embedded tool lifecycle catch path, including wrapped `UNAVAILABLE` gateway errors whose nested `nodeError.message` starts with a canonical denial token.
- Add regression coverage so prose-only denial text is not promoted to fatal, while structured denial signals still are.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron diagnostic classification no longer treats ordinary assistant/tool prose containing denial-looking phrases as a fatal cron failure unless structured error metadata marks the run as fatal. The follow-up patch also covers thrown gateway errors from the real lifecycle catch path, where `GatewayClientRequestError.gatewayCode`, nested `details.nodeError.code`, and nested `details.nodeError.message` must survive conversion into `tool_execution_end`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout in an isolated worktree on this branch, using the patched embedded tool lifecycle and cron failure-signal modules from the worktree root.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e '<build a thrown gateway/node-host denial, convert it through buildToolLifecycleErrorResult, extract the structured code, and resolve the cron failure signal>'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/failure-signal.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:changed
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the one-shot runtime probe:

```json
{
  "lifecycleStatus": "error",
  "wrapperCode": "UNAVAILABLE",
  "nestedNodeCode": "UNAVAILABLE",
  "nestedNodeMessage": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required",
  "extractedErrorCode": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED",
  "fatalForCron": true,
  "failureSignalCode": "SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED",
  "failureSignalMessage": "UNAVAILABLE: SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval required"
}
```

Observed result after fix: prose-only `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED` / `could not run` text remains normal cron output, prose-only tool output does not synthesize an error code, typed structured denial metadata still produces a fatal cron failure, and thrown gateway/node-host denials now normalize the production shape where both wrapper and nested codes are `UNAVAILABLE` while the nested message starts with `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED`.

What was not tested: full baseline `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; this used the focused core lane for the touched classifier and tool-error lifecycle surfaces. No production cron job was modified.

Supplemental checks:

- Focused Vitest lane passed: 6 files, 145 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed the core/coreTests lane, lint, typechecks, dependency guards, import-cycle guard, and related changed-file checks.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md` was updated so the documented cron contract matches the structured-only classifier plus node-host wrapper compatibility.

## Review

`codex review --base origin/main` was attempted twice locally, but the local Codex CLI failed before review started with OpenAI API 401 auth errors. Manual review caught and fixed one extraction bug before opening the PR, and the ClawSweeper/Codex review finding about thrown gateway errors is addressed in the latest commit.

## AI-assisted

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the diff, understand the code path, and ran the proof above locally.
target.author: abnershang
